explanation: The natural language requirement is about ensuring that user-set ventilation parameters, alarm thresholds, and calibration parameters are persistently logged. This is so that, in case of a power loss, those parameters can be restored and ventilation can resume with the same settings. The rationale text also emphasizes the need for these parameters to be restorable. The atomic propositions available that correspond to logging these entities are logVentilationParams (for user-set ventilation parameters), logAlarmParams or logAlarmSettings (for alarm thresholds), and logCalibrationParams (for calibration parameters). Since these logs should always occur (persistent requirement), the 'always satisfy ...' template is most appropriate. The boolean expression is the conjunction of all three atomic propositions, as all these loggings are required.
